summary: Small fighter decomp matching PR for ftCo_DamageFall: added a local FtMotionId copy in ftCo_80090594 before calling calcShift, then changed configure.py to link ftCo_DamageFall.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.

key_files.role: Matched source file; ftCo_80090594 gained a local FtMotionId motion_id initialized from msid and calcShift now uses that local.
Build configuration changed ftCo_DamageFall.c from NonMatching to Matching.

what_changed.text: In ftCo_80090594, introduced `FtMotionId motion_id = msid;` at function start.
Changed the calcShift call from `calcShift(hitlag_mul, msid, dmg)` to `calcShift(hitlag_mul, motion_id, dmg)`.
Updated configure.py so `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageFall.c` is listed as `Object(Matching, ...)` instead of `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.

smart_moves.text: Kept the code change minimal and localized to a single parameter-copy/codegen shape in ftCo_80090594 before flipping the object to Matching.

decomp_lessons.text: For this function, preserving a separate typed local copy of a parameter (`FtMotionId motion_id = msid`) was part of the source shape used when linking the TU as Matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftCo_DamageFall.c local copy and calcShift use

naming_conventions.text: The new local is named `motion_id`, matching its `FtMotionId` type and the existing parameter role `msid`.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: A narrow parameter-to-local copy can be a matching tactic when the function needs a distinct local lifetime/register shape; here `msid` was copied to `motion_id` and the helper call used the local.

notes: The changed files and exact code edits are well supported by the diff.
There is no PR body, review comment, or explicit objdiff/checkdiff transcript in the loaded slice.
The configure.py change to `Object(Matching, ...)` supports that the PR linked the object as Matching, but the slice does not include independent verifier output.
No reviewer rationale or acceptance reasoning is available beyond the merged PR metadata.

accepted_candidate_records.title: ftCo_80090594 used a typed local copy of msid before matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2597, ftCo_80090594 in ftCo_DamageFall.c added `FtMotionId motion_id = msid;` and passed `motion_id` to `calcShift`; the same PR changed ftCo_DamageFall.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. Treat as a localized matching tactic, not a global rule that parameters should be copied.

rejection_notes: Do not promote this PR into a broad standard that all FtMotionId parameters should be copied to locals; evidence is a single function edit.
Do not claim an objdiff/checkdiff validation transcript from this slice; only the Matching configure.py flip and merged metadata are present.
No review feedback was present, so no reviewer preference or intent should be inferred.
